claim: Systematic replication of Hand A's morphological characterisation on Hand B via 5 parallel sub-tests. The batch-level outcome reports which sub-tests confirm the Hand A pattern, which diverge, and which are inconclusive — establishing a parallel evidence base for Hand B equivalent to v2's Hand A coverage.
evidence_for: Each sub-test uses methodology identical to its Hand A equivalent with only the corpus filter changed.
Each sub-test has its own pre-registration and execution commit per pre-registration discipline.
Methodology lesson 9 (always run parallel tests on companion corpora) and lesson 10 (sample-composition parity) both directly support this batch design.
evidence_against: Some Hand A tests had Hand-A-specific values (e.g. AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01 used Hand A's specific M1-M5 values). The Hand B parallel must use Hand-B-specific values, which means the test isn't strictly identical — the methodology is identical but the inputs are hand-specific.
Five sub-tests with five separate verdicts can produce mixed outcomes that don't cleanly map to the user's three-bin expected categories (all-confirm / 3-4-confirm / 0-2-confirm).
test:
BATCH-LEVEL DESCRIPTION (each sub-test has its own LOCKED PROCEDURE in its own hypothesis file):

  Sub-test 1a: H-BV-HAND-B-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 — 5-criterion two-layer morphology test on Hand B
  Sub-test 1b: H-BV-HAND-B-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 — V/C sub-class partition + M2 outer-preference table on Hand B
  Sub-test 1c: H-BV-HAND-B-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 — k-means clustering of stems by vowel-inner distribution + harmony prediction
  Sub-test 1d: H-BV-HAND-B-AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01 — 7-language profile-distance ranking on Hand B M1-M5 values
  Sub-test 1e: H-BV-HAND-B-BASQUE-DIRECT-CORPUS-01 — Hand B values vs UD_Basque-BDT direct measurements

  BATCH-LEVEL VERDICT INTERPRETATION (LOCKED, descriptive per user's spec):
    For each sub-test, classify outcome as CONFIRM_HAND_A_PATTERN, DIVERGE_FROM_HAND_A, or INCONCLUSIVE.
    Count confirmations vs divergences across the 5 sub-tests.
    Specialist interpretation per user's expected outcomes:
      All 5 confirm: unified morphological system across hands
      3-4 confirm: largely unified with specific divergences
      0-2 confirm: structurally different systems
test_threshold: All 5 confirm = unified; 3-4 confirm = largely unified; 0-2 confirm = structurally different.

sub_tests.note: Identical 4/5 with C4 ordering as sole failure (HB ratio 1.14 vs HA 1.12)

sub_tests.verdict: MARGINAL with n vowel-exclusivity replicated (4/5 outers >=3x V/C; ol differs)
sub_tests.note: n vowel-exclusive in both hands (HA 0/2240, HB 0/7403); ol selectional pattern Hand-A-specific

sub_tests.verdict: CONFIRMED_HARMONY (silhouette 0.6495 + accuracy 0.7674); Hand A REFUTED at silhouette 0.3305
sub_tests.note: MAJOR divergence — Hand B shows clean vowel harmony Hand A lacks

sub_tests.verdict: NOT_BASQUE_FIRST (Ainu rank 1 dist 0.154; Basque rank 3 dist 0.333)
sub_tests.note: Hand A: Basque uniquely best dist 0.0. Hand B: Ainu best, Basque rank 3
Hand B fits fewer Basque metrics; M4 (Hand A boundary fit) now misses; M5 closer than Hand A but still outside band

tests_run.batch_verdict_note: Per the user-specified specialist interpretation: 0-2 confirm = structurally different systems (significant re-interpretation needed). The two-layer terminal morphology and V/C sub-class structure are SHARED (sub-tests 1a, 1b). But vowel harmony, candidate-language ranking, and direct-corpus Basque fit all DIVERGE between hands. The hands appear to share basic morphological architecture (closed inventories, productive stems, V/C sub-slots, n vowel-exclusivity) but diverge on typological-feature coupling (harmony, candidate language, Basque fit). This is consistent with: (a) two related dialects of the same language with different content domains and harmony rules; (b) two different scribes encoding different content with different conventions; (c) the same language at two different layers of structural abstraction where one shows harmony and the other doesn't.

verdict_note: Batch verdict: 2/5 sub-tests CONFIRM Hand A pattern, 3/5 DIVERGE. Per user-specified interpretation: 0-2 confirm = structurally different systems requiring significant re-interpretation. Sharing basic morphological architecture (sub-tests 1a/1b) but diverging on harmony, candidate language, and Basque fit (1c/1d/1e).
findings_for_followup: CRITICAL FOR v2 (or v3): Hand B is NOT just 'Hand A with different scribal parameters.' The two hands diverge on three load-bearing typological metrics (vowel harmony, candidate-language ranking, direct-corpus Basque fit). The v2 framing of 'Currier A/B is partly section-confounded; within-section the hands converge' (from H-BV-HAND-B-SECTION-BREAKDOWN-01) holds for SURFACE statistics (TTR, mean length) but NOT for deep typological features.

VOWEL HARMONY DIVERGENCE (1c): Hand B silhouette 0.65 vs Hand A 0.33. Cluster structure clean: BACK cluster a-preferring (mean p(a)=0.68), FRONT cluster e-preferring (mean p(e)=0.69). Stem-internal vowel composition predicts cluster at 77% accuracy. This is the SHARPEST cross-hand divergence of the batch.

CANDIDATE LANGUAGE DIVERGENCE (1d): Hand A pointed to Basque (uniquely best, distance 0.0). Hand B points to Ainu (rank 1, distance 0.154); Basque rank 3 (distance 0.333). With Hand B confirmed harmonic in 1c, the natural follow-up is to expand the candidate set to include harmonic agglutinatives (Hungarian, Turkish, Finnish) for Hand B specifically.

BASQUE FIT WEAKER (1e): Hand B fits 2/6 UD_Basque metrics (Hand A: 3/6). M4 gap density crosses out of Basque tolerance (0.26 vs band [0.067, 0.20]). The Basque-congruent finding for Hand A does NOT extend to Hand B at the corpus-direct level.

SHARED FEATURES (1a/1b): Both hands have 4/5 SUFFIX-SEQUENCE criteria pass with identical patterns (closed inventories, productive stems, high coverage, weak ordering at ratio ~1.13). Both hands have V/C sub-class structure with n vowel-exclusivity (categorical 0/2240 in A, 0/7403 in B). The basic morphological architecture is shared.

INTERPRETATION CANDIDATES: (a) Two related dialects of the same language, with Hand B retaining vowel harmony Hand A has lost (or vice versa); (b) Same language but different content domains where Hand B's section diversity reveals harmony Hand A's herbal sample masks; (c) Different authors/scribes with different encoding conventions; (d) Two different languages sharing surface morphological architecture (a typological convergence rather than genetic relation).

v3 NARRATIVE OUTLINE: the 'unified morphology + section-confounded A/B' framing from v2 is too simple. The hands share surface morphological architecture but diverge on deep typological features. v3 should distinguish 'shared architecture' findings (v2 carryover) from 'hand-specific typological signature' findings (this batch). The Basque-best finding from v2 is now Hand-A-specific.

FOLLOW-UP CANDIDATES: (1) Re-run AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION on Hand B with harmonic candidates added; (2) Direct corpus comparison against UD_Hungarian or UD_Turkish for Hand B; (3) Per-section analysis of vowel harmony within Hand B (does it concentrate in biological/recipes/herbal differently?); (4) Test whether Hand A would show harmony if restricted to its non-herbal sub-corpus.
reasoning: Batch hypothesis containing 5 parallel sub-tests applied to Hand B with methodology identical to Hand A equivalents. The batch-level outcome answers whether Hand A's morphological characterisation extends to Hand B. Per the v2 literature audit and methodology lesson 9, parallel testing on companion corpora is a discipline requirement before manuscript-wide claims.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-18 user-directed batch H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01 systematic Hand B replication.
